title: еҹәдәҺе·Іжңүиө„ж–ҷеҲҶжһҗжҠҖжңҜи·ҜзәҝдёҺдә§е“Ғе·®и·қ

Epic: Implement DualDaemon mutual heartbeat monitoring
description:
Implement mutual UDP heartbeat monitoring between Daemon 1 and Daemon 2:

- Daemon 1 listens on UDP 10001, sends to UDP 10002
- Daemon 2 listens on UDP 10002, sends to UDP 10001
- Heartbeat interval: 5 seconds
- Miss threshold: 3 times (15 seconds)
- Auto-restart peer if heartbeat lost

Heartbeat message includes:
- daemonId (1 or 2)
- pid
- sequence number
- timestamp
- status (httpPort, wsPort, uptime)

title: Implement UDP heartbeat listener in DualDaemon
description:
Add UDP socket listener for receiving peer heartbeat messages:
- Create startHeartbeatListener(daemon, port) method
- Parse JSON heartbeat messages
- Update daemon.lastHeartbeatSequence
- Reset heartbeatMissedCount on receive

title: Implement UDP heartbeat broadcaster in DualDaemon
description:
Add UDP socket broadcaster for sending heartbeat to peer:
- Create startHeartbeatBroadcaster(daemon, targetPort) method
- Send heartbeat messages every HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS
- Include daemonId, pid, sequence, timestamp, status
- Use localhost broadcast to target port

title: Start monitored Project Agents on Server startup
description:
On Server startup:
1. Read registry.monitored list
2. For each monitored project, start Orchestrator agent
3. Agent auto-registers via register_agent tool
4. System Agent can query via get_registered_agents tool

title: SessionManager.deleteSession еә”еҗҢж—¶жё…зҗҶ ledger ж•°жҚ®е’ҢеҒңжӯў kernel agent
UI session еҲ—иЎЁж”№дёәеҹәдәҺ ledger зӣ®еҪ•жү«жҸҸиҖҢйқһ session.json
UI еҢәеҲҶ ledger(жҢҒд№…еҢ–зңҹжәҗ) е’Ң dynamic session(еҠЁжҖҒжӢјжҺҘзҡ„дёҠдёӢж–Ү)
Ledger embedding hybrid recall for context builder
description: Add embedding-based hybrid recall to ledger/context builder. Reuse existing embedding adapter + vector store so compact/task summaries can be semantically retrieved before model rerank. Keep ledger as SSOT, preserve explainable slot/time results, and support no-tag tasks via summary/content semantic retrieval.
notes: Implemented session-local task embedding index + hybrid recall in context builder; added unit coverage and passed targeted context-builder + tsc validation.
close_reason: Implemented session-local task embedding index + hybrid recall for context builder. Verified with targeted context-builder tests (including new embedding recall unit) and pnpm -s tsc --noEmit.

title: Epic: Tag-aggregated context builder with task-end tagging
description: Implement tag-aggregated context building without session switching. Scope includes: fixing contextHistoryProvider to use external/response sessionId, persisting task-end summary tags/topic into ledger/mailbox metadata, and enhancing context builder ranking to prioritize tag matching over content relevance and time proximity while rebuilding in-session history from ledger.

title: Fix: contextHistoryProvider uses external sessionId to avoid runtime session miss
description: KernelAgentBase should call contextHistoryProvider with response/external session id instead of internal memory session id, so runtime.getSession(sessionId) can resolve and context builder can execute.

title: Task-end tagging pipeline for session classification
Tag-aware context builder: enhance ranking with ledger tag extraction
Epic: Ledger-only persistence + dynamic session views
description: Migrate Finger from session-file-centric persistence to ledger-only persistence plus dynamic session views. Scope: keep sessionId as logical scope only, remove session files as message truth, split model-visible context into working set (current turn reasoning zone) and historical memory zone, and require overflow history to be fetched via context_ledger.memory instead of forced prompt injection. Follow docs/design/ledger-only-dynamic-session-views.md as design source of truth.
notes: еӯҗд»»еҠЎ262.1/262.2/262.3/262.4еқҮе·Іе®ҢжҲҗпјҢledger-only persistence + dynamic session viewsж ёеҝғж”№йҖ й—ӯзҺҜе®ҢжҲҗгҖӮ

title: Promote context_ledger.memory as overflow-history retrieval path
description: Upgrade context_ledger.memory to serve as the canonical overflow-history retrieval path for budget-excluded ledger history. Follow-up scope includes better recall/search semantics, slot/detail fetch, and explainable mapping between context builder omissions and ledger tool retrieval.

title: Update prompts to teach incomplete-context + ledger retrieval model
description: Update system/developer/skills prompt layers so the model explicitly knows: current context is not the full history, working set and historical memory are budgeted views, ledger is the source of truth, and when evidence is missing it must actively query context_ledger.memory instead of guessing.

title: Split context builder into working set and historical memory zone
description: Refactor context builder to explicitly construct two model-visible regions: (1) working set/current-turn reasoning zone with guaranteed retention, and (2) historical memory zone under budget control. Ensure current task never competes with historical recall and metadata/monitoring expose the boundary clearly.
close_reason: Implemented explicit working_set vs historical_memory zone split in context builder. Current task no longer competes with historical recall; build metadata and context monitor/session-view mappings now expose zone counts/tokens and per-message contextZone. Verified with targeted context-builder tests and pnpm -s tsc --noEmit.

title: Remove session files as message source of truth
description: Audit and refactor all runtime/server/UI paths that still treat persisted session files or session.messages as message truth. Ledger must be the only persisted message source; session metadata may remain, but message history must be reconstructed from ledger-derived views only.
notes: Ledger-only session source-of-truthж”¶еҸЈе®ҢжҲҗпјҡsession/messagesеҺ»зңҹжәҗпјҢserver/messagehub/ledger routesж”№дёәledger snapshotпјӣtsc+е…ій”®еӣһеҪ’жөӢиҜ•йҖҡиҝҮгҖӮ

acceptance_criteria: 1) shadow иҫ“еҮәе·®ејӮеҸҜе®Ўи®Ўпјӣ2) еҲҮжөҒеҗҺжё йҒ“ж¶ҲжҒҜдёҚеӣһйҖҖпјӣ3) еӣһеҪ’йҖҡиҝҮ systemвҶ’projectвҶ’review е…Ёй“ҫи·ҜгҖӮ

title: EndTurn marker gate + optional review lifecycle closeout
description: Implement application-layer stop control via explicit reasoning.stop tool. Add runtime switch, prompt injection guidance, stop-gate continuation, and finalization gate to avoid premature end-turn on finish_reason=stop.

title: Context history digest + rebuild guard + timeout tracing
description: Design source: docs/design/context-history-digest-and-rebuild-guard.md. Scope: 1) rebuild trigger hardening (only empty bootstrap or explicit BU), 2) preserve non-history prompt injections, 3) task digest history view + expand_task full-text recovery, 4) timeout tracing for 'chat-codex timed out after 600000ms'.

title: Enforce rebuild trigger invariants (only empty bootstrap or explicit BU)
description: Remove/disable implicit history rebuild paths when history is non-empty. Keep unfinished-turn continuation on locked history; allow rebuild only for bootstrap-empty or explicit context_builder.rebuild/overflow policy with trace evidence.

title: Stabilize non-history prompt injections (skills/mailbox/flow) across rebuild/no-rebuild
description: Add/adjust tests proving skills/mailbox/flow/system/developer prompt injections are independent from history provider and context rebuild decisions.

title: Introduce task digest history view (request+finish summary+slot range)
description: Add digest transformation layer for history injection. Use task as minimum unit; inject chronological digest entries under budget instead of full task transcripts.

title: Add context_ledger.expand_task wrapper tool for full-text recovery
description: Expose an explicit expand tool to fetch full ledger entries for one digest item by task_id or slot range, delegating to context_ledger.memory query detail mode.

title: Add timeout error tracing for chat-codex 600000ms failures
description: Track and expose timeout chain with session_id/agent_id/thread_key/turn_id/dispatch_id/flow_id/seq/history_source/history_items_count/context_tokens. Ensure user-facing error includes timeout reason and recovery state.

title: End-to-end acceptance and regression suite for digest+expand+timeout
description: Cover: no implicit rebuild on unfinished continuation, stable injections, digest injection format, expand full text, and timeout tracing visibility in logs/events/channel.

title: Multi-agent prompt and collaboration hardening (Claude-inspired, Finger-adapted)
description: Land prompt contracts + runtime guardrails to enforce System/Project/Reviewer boundaries, structured task-report protocol, continuation lane, and restart-safe control-plane lifecycle. Canonical lifecycle remains in docs/design/project-task-lifecycle-state-machine.md; execution plan in docs/design/multi-agent-prompt-collaboration-hardening-epic.md.
acceptance_criteria:
1) System does not re-implement delegated in-flight project tasks.
2) Reviewer cannot dispatch implementation work.
3) Project reject loop returns to project only.
4) PASS enters pending_approval then final closure by system.
5) Restart resumes bound session without duplicate dispatch.
notes: Epic completed: prompt hardening, structured report contract, reviewer runtime dispatch forbid, session binding guard, continuation lane (agent.continue), and dispatch-graph durability with restart-safe persistence + regression tests.

title: Prompt hardening: System/Project/Reviewer contract rewrite
description: Update role prompts to enforce strict collaboration boundaries and dispatch/update/review behavior.
acceptance_criteria: 1) System prompt enforces pre-dispatch status gate and monitor-only mode after dispatch.\n2) Project prompt enforces evidence-backed completion claims.\n3) Reviewer prompt enforces PASS/REJECT only and no execution dispatch.
notes: Completed prompt hardening pass. Updated: src/agents/finger-system-agent/system-prompt.md, src/agents/finger-system-agent/system-dev-prompt.md, src/agents/chat-codex/dev-prompts/executor.md, src/agents/chat-codex/dev-prompts/reviewer.md. Added deterministic pre-dispatch gate, strict monitor-only behavior after dispatch, reviewer hard execution boundary, and executor delivery-claim contract.

title: Structured report contract for project/reviewer completion
description: Replace text-only completion forwarding with structured payload schema and parser integration.
acceptance_criteria: 1) task-report payload is structured (taskId/taskName/status/evidence/next_action).\n2) Runtime no longer depends on free-form summary parsing for routing decisions.\n3) Backward compatibility shim validated for one cycle.
notes: Implemented structured task report contract (finger.task-report.v1) across report-task-completion and task-report-dispatcher; added deterministic delivery-claim routing via structured fields (delivery_claim/status/next_action) with legacy text fallback; attached structured payload in dispatch metadata.taskReport; added/updated unit tests and validated with vitest + tsc --noEmit.

title: Add continuation lane for in-flight task/session
description: Introduce continue semantics so in-flight task follow-ups do not create duplicate dispatch identities.
acceptance_criteria: 1) New continue path exists for same task/session.\n2) Duplicate redispatch rate drops in regression tests.\n3) project.task.update remains only for explicit requirement changes.
notes: Completed continuation lane: added agent.continue runtime tool for in-flight task/session continuation (same task identity + bound session), reviewer-forbidden guard, and tests in tests/unit/server/agent-runtime-continue-tool.test.ts. Uses existing dispatch suppression/binding guards to avoid duplicate dispatch identities.

title: Reviewer runtime permission hardening
description: Enforce reviewer as validation-only role at runtime, not only by prompt text.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Reviewer cannot call agent.dispatch.\n2) Reviewer cannot transfer implementation ownership.\n3) Runtime returns explicit forbidden errors for violation attempts.
notes: Completed reviewer runtime permission hardening: (1) agent.dispatch now rejects reviewer callers with explicit forbidden error; (2) source_agent_id spoofing is rejected when caller context mismatches; (3) added unit guard tests in tests/unit/server/agent-runtime-dispatch-guard.test.ts; validated with targeted vitest and tsc --noEmit.

title: Session binding guard: immutable task-session tuple validation
description: Validate task/session binding tuple on dispatch/update/review actions and fail fast on scope mismatch.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Binding tuple persisted and validated.\n2) Mismatch actions are rejected with explicit errors.\n3) No cross-session contamination in restart/recovery regression tests.
notes: Completed session binding guard: projectTaskState/projectTaskRegistry now persist boundSessionId+revision; dispatch validates immutable active binding (task identity + bound session) and fails fast with explicit dispatch_binding_mismatch errors; added unit coverage in tests/modules/dispatch-task-to-agent.test.ts for taskId/session mismatch cases; validated with vitest + tsc --noEmit.

title: Control-plane durability: persist dispatch graph for restart recovery
description: Move critical dispatch relationship state from memory-only tracker to restart-safe control-plane persistence.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Parent-child dispatch graph survives restart.\n2) Cascade interrupt/recovery uses persisted graph.\n3) Existing behavior remains compatible during migration.
notes: Completed control-plane durability: DispatchTracker now persists dispatch graph to ~/.finger/runtime/schedules/dispatch-graph.json and reloads on startup; cleanup/complete sync persistence; added persistence + cascade-after-reload tests in tests/unit/server/dispatch-tracker-persistence.test.ts.

title: Project-scoped Dream Memory Orchestration
description: Implement project-isolated nightly dream consolidation: System agent orchestrates, Project agents execute project-scoped dream, outputs land in ~/.finger/memory/projects/<project_slug>/ as reusable rules/guardrails/playbooks (not daily report text). Design source: docs/design/project-dream-memory-orchestration.md
acceptance_criteria: 1) System nightly builds monitored+today-active project list and dispatches dream tasks asynchronously. 2) Each project dream writes only under ~/.finger/memory/projects/<project_slug>/ with lock/idempotency. 3) Outputs are high-signal assets (rules/guardrails/playbooks/delivery patterns), not pure log summaries. 4) Structured observability and failure-recovery are implemented. 5) End-to-end replay proves multi-project parallel dream execution without cross-project memory pollution.
notes: All child tasks closed: nightly list+dispatch, lock/idempotency, project memory atomic writer, observability/recovery, and replay validation completed with tests and backend build pass.

title: Project dream runner with lock/idempotency
description: Implement project-scoped dream execution path in Project Agent: acquire per-project lock, digest-first retrieval, noise filtering, targeted detail expansion, idempotent run handling.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Lock file prevents concurrent dream on same project. 2) Re-entrant same run_id does not duplicate writes. 3) digest-first + targeted detail expansion implemented. 4) Lock released on success/failure.
notes: Implemented project dream lock lifecycle: per-project .dream.lock with TTL/reentrant protection; scheduler acquires lock before dispatch and skips lock-busy/reentrant; immediate dispatch fail/completed paths release lock; nightly task report path releases lock on terminal success/failure. Added tests: tests/unit/core/project-dream-lock.test.ts, tests/unit/server/heartbeat-scheduler-nightly-dream.test.ts, tests/unit/tools/internal/report-task-completion-tool.test.ts.

title: E2E replay validation for multi-project dream isolation
description: Build integration validation that triggers dream on multiple projects and verifies isolation + output quality contract.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Replay proves two+ projects can run dream in parallel. 2) Memory outputs stay isolated by project_slug. 3) Output classification contains rule/guardrail/playbook/delivery-pattern categories. 4) Evidence logs captured for success and one induced failure case.
notes: Added replay validation: tests/integration/nightly-project-dream-isolation-replay.test.ts verifies multi-project parallel dream memory writes remain isolated by project slug, checks output category signals (rule/guardrail/playbook/delivery pattern), and includes induced failure case proving successful project output is preserved.

title: Nightly project list builder + async dream dispatch
description: System Agent nightly window builds de-duplicated project list from monitored projects + today active projects, then dispatches project dream tasks asynchronously (non-blocking).
acceptance_criteria: 1) List source includes monitored + today-active. 2) De-dupe by project_slug. 3) Dispatch is async and does not block on single project completion. 4) Dispatch status persisted for each project.
close_reason: Implemented nightly project list builder + async dream dispatch in HeartbeatScheduler; added monitored+today-active de-dup, once-per-day project dispatch state persistence, and runtime-state persistence fields. Verified with targeted tests and backend build.

title: Project memory storage layout + atomic writer
description: Implement project memory path layout under ~/.finger/memory/projects/<project_slug>/ and atomic writer for MEMORY.md + memories/*.md updates.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Writes constrained to project-scoped directory. 2) MEMORY.md index and topic files are updated atomically via temp+rename. 3) Cross-project write attempts are rejected. 4) State file (.dream.state.json) persists last successful run metadata.
notes: Implemented project-scoped memory writer with atomic temp+rename writes: src/core/project-dream-memory-store.ts. Nightly dream report now writes MEMORY.md + memories/<date>-<hash>.md + .dream.state.json under ~/.finger/memory/projects/<slug>/, then reports to system. Added tests: tests/unit/core/project-dream-memory-store.test.ts (including path normalization to keep writes under project root).

title: Dream observability + failure recovery
description: Add structured logs/metrics for dream lifecycle and retry/recovery handling without blocking other projects.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Structured fields include dream_run_id/project_slug/source/status/duration/change counts. 2) Failure path supports bounded retries and records failure reason. 3) One project failure does not block others in same nightly batch. 4) Aggregated batch summary is available for System Agent reporting.
notes: Added nightly dream observability + recovery: scheduler now logs structured fields (dream_run_id/source/status/duration), supports bounded dispatch retries (maxDispatchRetries/retryBackoffMs), records per-project failure reasons, and emits batch summary (candidates/dispatched/failed/skipped). Added tests for retry and failure-isolation in tests/unit/server/heartbeat-scheduler-nightly-dream.test.ts; nightly terminal report logs change counts in report-task-completion path.

title: Structured Control Contract + Memory Hook Runtime (Phase 1)
description: Implement docs/design/structured-control-contract-and-rebuild.md as runtime truth for per-turn control_block, hook execution, USER/FLOW/MEMORY update routing, and rebuild selection behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
1) control_block contract parsed and validated each turn
2) hook table executes deterministic actions for profile/flow/memory/rebuild triggers
3) USER.md is injected in runtime context each turn; FLOW remains injected; MEMORY retrieval path is prompt-enforced
4) daily review path follows append-only + configurable backup
5) end-to-end tests cover success/failure/degraded paths

title: Implement control_block parser + compatibility guard
description: Add parser/validator for structured control block with forward-compatible fields; enforce required baseline fields and soft-fail on unknown extensions.
acceptance_criteria: Parser validates required fields; unknown fields preserved; invalid payload emits explicit error signal without crashing turn loop.

title: Implement hook executor for profile/flow/memory/rebuild actions
description: Map control fields to deterministic runtime hooks: user profile append, flow patch append, memory patch append, digest policy, rebuild trigger.
acceptance_criteria: Hook mapping table exists; each hook has idempotency key; failures are isolated and logged; turn continues.

title: Wire dynamic prompt injection: USER/FLOW + memory routing instructions
description: Ensure USER.md and FLOW.md runtime injection are stable; inject memory retrieval routing policy for MEMORY/ledger/slots/expand/rebuild usage.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt contains USER and FLOW blocks; memory routing policy visible in system/developer prompt; tests pass.

title: Implement append-only daily update + configurable Obsidian backup
description: Enforce append-only semantics for daily review updates and support configurable local/Obsidian backup paths with graceful degrade.
acceptance_criteria: append-only violations are blocked; backup path is configurable and normalized; backup failure does not break main flow.

title: Add test suite for structured control lifecycle
description: Add unit/integration tests for parser, hook execution, rebuild trigger, and daily backup flow including error isolation behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover success + failure + degraded scenarios; CI test command green.

notes:

ж–Үд»¶иҜ»еҸ–жҲҗеҠҹ: /Volumes/extension/code/finger/docs/NETWORK_ARCHITECTURE.md # зҪ‘з»ңжһ¶жһ„и®ҫи®ЎпјҡиҒ”зҪ‘еҜ№жҲҳдёҺдәәжңәеҜ№жҲҳ  ## 1. жҰӮиҝ°  жң¬ж–ҮжЎЈе®ҡд№ү Finger йЎ№зӣ®зҡ„иҒ”зҪ‘еҜ№жҲҳе’ҢдәәжңәеҜ№жҲҳзҡ„йҖҡдҝЎеҚҸи®®гҖҒзҠ¶жҖҒеҗҢжӯҘжңәеҲ¶гҖҒеҢ№й…Қзі»з»ҹи®ҫи®ЎгҖӮ  ### 1.1 и®ҫи®Ўзӣ®ж Ү  - **дҪҺе»¶иҝҹ**пјҡзҠ¶жҖҒеҗҢжӯҘе»¶иҝҹ < 100msпјҲеұҖеҹҹзҪ‘пјү/ < 200msпјҲе…¬зҪ‘пјү - **е…¬е№іжҖ§**пјҡйҳІжӯўдҪңејҠпјҢзЎ®дҝқжүҖжңүе®ўжҲ·з«ҜзҠ¶жҖҒдёҖиҮҙ - **еҸҜжү©еұ•**пјҡж”ҜжҢҒ 1v1гҖҒеӨҡдәәж··жҲҳгҖҒдәәжңәеҜ№жҲҳзӯүеӨҡз§ҚжЁЎејҸ - **е®№й”ҷжҖ§**пјҡзҪ‘з»ңжіўеҠЁж—¶иҮӘеҠЁжҒўеӨҚпјҢж–ӯзәҝйҮҚиҝһ  ---  ## 2. жһ¶жһ„жҖ»и§Ҳ  ``` в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ
